channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Tier 1 geopolitical news headlines curated by Frontion

item.title: Ukraine running critically low on air defence missiles after winter campaign
item.description: Ukraine's air force warned it is running short of air defence missiles following Russia's massive winter attack campaign, as Kyiv braces for further strikes amid ongoing conflict.

item.title: Zelenskiy confirms US-brokered three-day ceasefire and prisoner exchange with Russia
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy confirmed a three-day ceasefire arranged with Russia as part of US efforts to negotiate an end to the over four-year-old war.

item.title: Virginia Supreme Court throws out Democratic congressional map, boosts Republican midterm hopes
item.description: The court rejected a Democratic-backed ballot measure that reconfigured the state's US House districts for partisan advantage, dealing a major blow to Democratic midterm prospects.

item.title: US trade chief says Trump administration will win appeal on temporary tariffs ruling
item.description: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said the administration expects to prevail in appealing the Court of International Trade's ruling against its temporary 10% global tariffs.

item.title: US and Iran Remain Deadlocked Despite Ongoing Gulf Clashes
item.description: Washington awaits Tehran's response to a US proposal to formally end the war, but neither side appears closer to resolution as military confrontations continue in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Trump Administration Appeals Court Ruling Against Global Tariffs
item.description: The administration challenged a trade court decision that struck down the 10% global tariff imposed in February, with the US Trade Representative expressing confidence in winning the appeal.

item.title: US Warns EU of Higher Tariffs if Trade Deal Misses July 4 Deadline
item.description: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer stated that the US will revert to higher tariffs on European goods if Brussels fails to implement trade commitments by the July 4 deadline.

item.title: India appoints new Chief of Defence Staff amid major military reforms
item.description: Retired Lt. Gen. NS Raja Subramani named India's next Chief of Defence Staff, succeeding Anil Chauhan as the country undertakes its biggest military overhaul to modernize and integrate the army, navy, and air force.

item.title: Plaid Cymru wins most seats in Wales Senedd election, ready to form government
item.description: Rhun ap Iorwerth's Plaid Cymru won the most seats in the Welsh Parliament election, signalling a shift in Welsh politics as the party prepares to form a new government after a historic victory.

item.title: Russian casualties in Ukraine reach 1.34 million, says Kyiv
item.description: Ukraine's General Staff reports Russia has lost approximately 1,340,270 troops since the full-scale invasion began in February 2022, in one of the highest casualty tolls of any modern conflict.

item.title: Trump appeals court loss on tariffs as trade policy chaos deepens
item.description: The Trump administration appealed a court ruling that its 10% global tariff imposed in February was not justified, adding to uncertainty in global trade as tensions with China and other partners continue.

item.title: US Suspects Billions in Nvidia Chips Smuggled to China via Thailand
item.description: A firm linked to Thailand national AI initiative is suspected of helping smuggle Super Micro servers containing advanced Nvidia chips to China, Bloomberg reported. The alleged sanctions evasion highlights ongoing chip export control challenges.

item.title: Norway Holds Chinese Woman on Suspicion of Espionage
item.description: A Chinese woman will be held in police custody for up to four weeks while investigators probe suspected espionage activities in Norway, a court ruled Friday.

item.title: Greece Investigates Ukrainian Naval Drone Found on Island
item.description: Greek authorities are investigating a drone boat discovered by fishermen in a cave on the Ionian island of Lefkada. The drone was identified as Ukrainian-made, raising questions about its presence in Greek waters.

item.title: Venice Biennale Jury Resigns Over Israel and Russia Participation
item.description: Geopolitical tensions have spilled into the Venice Biennale contemporary art exhibition, with the jury resigning in protest over Israeli and Russian participation in this years edition.

item.title: Russia holds scaled-back Victory Day parade amid Ukraine war
item.description: Russia held its most scaled-back Victory Day parade in years due to threat of attack from Ukraine, where victory has proven elusive four years into the deadliest European conflict since WWII.

item.title: Trump allies tensions likely to outlast Iran war
item.description: President Trump pulled troops from Germany and downplayed Iran attacks on Gulf partners, signaling fraying ties with key allies that could outlast the conflict.

item.title: US strikes Iranian ships in Gulf of Oman
item.description: US Central Command released video showing F/A-18 Super Hornets using precision munitions against two Iranian tankers as fighting continues around the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Belarus Expands Military Infrastructure With Russian Coordination
item.description: Ukrainian border officials report Belarus is developing training grounds and logistics routes that could support Russian operations against Ukraine, though no immediate offensive threat exists.

item.title: Poland Signs Record €44 Billion EU Defense Loan
item.description: Poland becomes the largest beneficiary of the EU SAFE program, securing preferential loans for defense modernization as Europe accelerates rearmament amid Russian threat and concerns over US commitment.

item.title: Russia Still Outproducing EU Militarily, Defence Commissioner Warns
item.description: EU Defence Commissioner Andrius Kubilius says Russia produces more ammunition in three months than NATO produces in a year, urging European defense industry to scale up faster.

item.title: Global Cyberattack Disrupts Thousands of Schools and Universities Worldwide
item.description: Hacking group ShinyHunters claimed responsibility for a major ransomware attack on Canvas, the academic software platform used by approximately 9,000 educational institutions globally. Students across the US, Canada, Australia and Europe faced exam disruptions as ransom demands appeared on screens during final exam season.

item.title: Hungary Swears in New Prime Minister After Viktor Orban Era Ends
item.description: Peter Magyar of the Tisza party is being sworn in as Hungary's new prime minister after a landslide victory ended 16 years of Viktor Orban's rule. The new government faces economic challenges and must negotiate the release of €17 billion in frozen EU funds.

item.title: South African Opposition Calls for Ramaphosa Resignation After Court Ruling
item.description: South Africa's Constitutional Court ruled that parliament violated the constitution by blocking impeachment proceedings against President Cyril Ramaphosa over the Farmgate scandal involving stolen cash at his farm. Opposition leader Julius Malema called for Ramaphosa to resign.

item.title: Mystery Naval Drone With Explosives Found in Greek Waters
item.description: Greek military is examining an unmanned naval drone discovered by fishermen near the island of Lefkada. Reports suggest the vessel may be Ukrainian-made and could be linked to operations against Russia's "shadow fleet" or the broader Ukraine-Russia conflict.
